---
name: audit
description: Audit a project's secrets/conceal posture — config, file ownership/mode, broker presence, .mcp.json wiring, and whether the agent uid is actually denied. Use when the user says "audit secrets", "secrets status", "is my secrets safe set up", "check the conceal boundary", or asks whether the secrets are actually protected.
argument-hint: '[repo-dir]'
user-invocable: true
allowed-tools: Bash, Read
---

<!-- codex:plugin-root-preamble v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js; edit the codemod, then re-run -->

> **Resolve the plugin root before running any script below.** A codex shell
> can inherit a stale, set-but-WRONG `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` from its launcher,
> so a bare `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-…}` fallback is NOT safe — validate the
> value against this skill's own SKILL.md first. Set `SKILL_MD` to this
> file's absolute path (codex lists it next to this skill in your skills
> list; under Claude Code the env var is always correct and the fallback
> branch never runs):
>
> ```bash
> SKILL_MD="/absolute/path/to/this/SKILL.md"   # from your skills list
> if [ -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-/nonexistent}/skills/audit/SKILL.md" ] &&
>    [ "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/audit/SKILL.md" -ef "$SKILL_MD" ]; then
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT"                         # validated — use as-is
> else
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$SKILL_MD")/../.." && pwd)" # stale/unset — self-locate
> fi
> ```
>
> Wherever this skill writes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, substitute the validated
> `$PLUGIN_ROOT` value (shells do NOT share variables between tool calls —
> re-resolve or inline the resolved path in each command).

# Secrets / conceal audit

Run the status script and present its output plainly:
```bash
node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/heimdall-conceal-status.js" "<repo-dir-or-blank>"
```
It reports: config presence, each secrets file's owner/mode, broker binary
owner/mode, how many `.mcp.json` servers route through the broker, and a live
check of whether the agent uid can read the secrets (the decisive test).

If the agent uid can still read a secrets file, the OS boundary is NOT in effect
— tell the user to run `/heimdall:harden` (or the setup script with sudo). The
hook-level conceal guard may still be active even when the OS boundary is not.
